methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $98M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Helical Fusion is a Series B nuclear energy company founded by Takaya Taguchi and Junichi Miyazawa that has raised $98M to date. The valuation universe comprises 11 peers: 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private firms including Type One Energy, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power. The nuclear energy sector remains nascent and heterogeneous, limiting precise peer-based triangulation.

The stage-progression method is the primary applicable approach. Using total capital raised ($98M) as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement yields an implied valuation of $186M. A peer-median approach was not executable; while 8 same-sector comps exist, insufficient overlap on stage, funding tier, or technology substep prevented reliable median extraction without introducing bias across fundamentally different fusion pathways. Relying on all-sector nuclear medians would obscure material differences in capital intensity and development timelines.

The triangulation supports a valuation range of $158M (low) to $214M (high), centered on $186M. This single-method synthesis reflects limited data granularity; the range brackets reasonable upside and downside scenarios given stage-typical dilution and burn-rate uncertainty. Key uncertainties include the trajectory of regulatory approval timelines for helical confinement approaches and relative progress velocity versus other advanced reactor modalities in the peer set. Investors should treat this band as indicative pending deeper operational diligence.
